current_suggestion.pitch: Huberman sits in the gap between your health science channels (Attia, Ekberg) and your mental strength interests — he systematically covers neuroscience-backed protocols for focus, stress regulation, sleep, and performance that none of your current channels own as a primary lane. Unlike Saladino or Chaffee who argue from a dietary ideology, Huberman typically cites specific mechanistic studies on hormones, neurochemistry, and behavior, giving you a different evidentiary lens on some of the same longevity and metabolism questions. Format is mostly long-form solo deep-dives (90–120 min) with occasional expert interviews, consistently high production and citation density.
